name: Dulce Base
description: The Dulce Base is a purported secret underground facility beneath Archuleta Mesa near Dulce, New Mexico, allegedly operated jointly by humans and extraterrestrial beings. The theory, primarily circulating within UFO and conspiracy communities, claims it's a bio-genetic laboratory performing unethical experiments on humans and animals. Proponents cite leaked documents (often of dubious origin), eyewitness testimonies (mostly anonymous or unverifiable), and alleged whistleblowers like Phil Schneider (whose claims have been widely disputed) as evidence. They claim the base is multi-leveled, technologically advanced, and involved in a secret war between humans and aliens. Mainstream perspectives dismiss the Dulce Base as a hoax or urban legend. No credible physical evidence supports its existence. The geological surveys of the area show no signs of a large underground structure. Skeptics argue that the claims are based on misinterpretations, fabrications, and the psychological phenomenon of pareidolia (seeing patterns where none exist). The remoteness of the area, combined with the pre-existing conspiracy theories surrounding Area 51 and government secrecy, may have contributed to the enduring appeal of the Dulce Base myth. The story often incorporates elements of alien abduction narratives and fears of government control, making it a potent symbol of distrust in authority. It taps into anxieties about technological advancement and the potential for abuse of power.
subtitle: Secret underground alien-human facility

claims.claim: Dulce Base is a real, multi-level underground facility.
claims.evidence: Testimonies of alleged whistleblowers like Phil Schneider, leaked documents (often of questionable authenticity), and supposed satellite imagery.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence, inconsistencies in testimonies, absence of geological evidence, and the dubious nature of 'leaked' documents. Schneider's claims have been widely discredited.

claims.claim: The base conducts unethical bio-genetic experiments on humans and animals.
claims.evidence: Graphic descriptions of experiments provided by alleged whistleblowers, often involving alien technology and hybridization.
claims.criticism: No credible medical or scientific evidence to support these claims. Descriptions often resemble science fiction tropes rather than realistic scientific procedures.

claims.claim: Humans and extraterrestrials collaborate at the Dulce Base.
claims.evidence: Claims of alien involvement from alleged whistleblowers and UFO researchers, often linking it to the Grey aliens.
claims.criticism: Absence of credible evidence of extraterrestrial life or contact. Claims rely heavily on anecdotal evidence and unsubstantiated rumors.

keyFigures.name: Phil Schneider
keyFigures.role: Alleged whistleblower who described his experiences working at the Dulce Base.

keyFigures.name: Jason Colavito
keyFigures.role: Researcher and skeptic who has debunked many of the claims associated with the Dulce Base.

timeline.event: First emergence of Dulce Base conspiracy theories.
The Dulce Base theory continues to circulate online and in conspiracy communities.

sources.author: Jason Colavito
sources.work: Cult of Alien Gods: H.P. Lovecraft and Extraterrestrial Pop Culture
sources.title: Jason Colavito

sources.author: Greg Bishop
sources.work: Project Beta: The Story of Paul Bennewitz, National Security, and Close Encounters
sources.title: Greg Bishop
